blocks.text:
Synthetic coverage for the plot renderer.

## Parametric (a Lissajous curve, equal-aspect trajectory)

blocks.source:
mode: parametric
t: 0..2*pi
x(t) = cos(t)
y(t) = sin(2*t)
mark: (1, 0) label="t=0"
grid: true

mode: polar
theta: 0..2*pi
r(theta) = 1 + 0.5*cos(3*theta)
grid: true

blocks.text: ## Function with reference lines and custom labels

blocks.source:
x: -3..3
f(x) = tanh(x)
vline: 0
hline: 1
hline: -1
xlabel: "u"
ylabel: "\tanh u"
samples: 120
grid: true
mark: (1, 0.7616) label="P" color="#c0392b"

meta.solution:
Not a real problem: this fixture exists so the conformance corpus exercises the parametric
and polar plot modes (and the grid / reference-line / label directives) that the curated
examples do not otherwise reach, giving the JS plot renderer a full parity target.
meta.subject: math
meta.title: Plot mode coverage (conformance fixture)
